preamble: Vocalization of the voiceless dental fricative [θ] to the close back rounded vowel [u] or its corresponding semivowel [w] occurs in some English and Scots varieties as a form of extreme lenition and cluster simplification.

examples.note: Historical development in certain varieties where final dental fricatives vocalize before elision.
Historical weakening in specific environments leading to the formation of diphthongs (e.g., development of 'ow' in some words).
